message:
Trim unused wasm exports — slim 41 → 38.7 KB gz

Audited each cpp_* export against actual JS call sites across js/,
dist/codegen.mjs, and bin/capnwasm.mjs (the runtime codegen template).
Anything no JS path touches goes — every export costs both a name in the
export table and a function the linker can't gc-section away.

Removed:
- cpp_any_builder_data_size (no caller)
- cpp_any_list_size (subsumed; cpp_any_open_list returns the size)
- cpp_out_capacity (output buffer cap is the same as input; never queried)
- cpp_rpc_build_finish (JS-side template builds Finish frames; saves a
  wasm boundary call + a MallocMessageBuilder cycle per RPC reply)
- cpp_rpc_get_call_question_id / interface_id / method_id / target_id
  (subsumed by cpp_rpc_get_call_summary which packs them all)
- cpp_rpc_get_return_answer_id (subsumed by cpp_rpc_get_release_id, which
  shares the same underlying function)
- cpp_rpc_get_return_cap_count (subsumed by cpp_rpc_get_return_summary)

Kept (a near-miss the test suite caught):
- cpp_any_builder_set_uint16 — codegen's runtime emits this for union
  discriminants. Not in any committed .gen.mjs because none of those
  schemas have unions.
- cpp_any_builder_set_uint8 / uint32 / int64_lo_hi / bool — used by the
  hand-coded browser-codegen demo (bench/codegen-in-browser.mjs) and by
  any user reaching for the builder API directly.

139 tests pass. Slim wasm: 109 → 99.8 KB raw, 41 → 38.7 KB gz across the
three bundle-shrinkage passes (this one + drop --export-dynamic + KJ
assertion-string stripping).

files.patch:
@@ -173,12 +173,21 @@ Linked from the README's documentation index.
 
 ## 8. Bundle-size headline
 
-44 KB gz vs capnweb's 21 KB. Could shave another 5–8 KB by stripping more KJ
-debug strings (the data section has assertion expression text), splitting the
-WASI shim further, replacing some C++ inner loops with WAT.
-
-Diminishing returns; below ~35 KB there's nothing major to grab without
-rewriting the wasm in hand-tuned WAT — a lot of maintenance for a couple of KB.
+**Update (2026-04-30):** down from 44 → 38.7 KB gz across three passes:
+- Drop `--export-dynamic` from the wasm link, keeping only the explicit
+  `--export=cpp_*` list (saved ~200 bytes gz).
+- Override KJ_REQUIRE / KJ_ASSERT macros to elide stringified condition +
+  message text (saved ~2 KB gz; see `cpp/kj_strip_strings.h`).
+- Audit the export list against actual JS call sites and remove the ones
+  no JS path touches: `cpp_any_list_size` (subsumed by open_list's return
+  value), `cpp_out_capacity` (the output buffer cap is the same as input
+  and never queried), `cpp_rpc_build_finish` (JS-side template builds
+  Finish frames), several per-call-field RPC getters that the
+  per-summary getter packs into one call (~300 bytes gz).
+
+Still on the list: 38.7 KB → ~35 KB needs structural changes — splitting
+the WASI shim further, replacing some C++ hot inner loops with hand-tuned
+WAT. Diminishing returns past that without rewriting in WAT.
 
 ## 9. CI / publishing automation — scaffolded
